# Daily Report — 2026-05-23

## Task Completed
Added `delete_incident` tool to complete incident CRUD in ServiceNow MCP. The tool accepts an incident number (e.g. INC0010001) or a sys_id, resolves the number to sys_id when needed via a GET lookup, then issues DELETE on `/api/now/table/incident/{sys_id}`. Returns a success response on HTTP 204/200, a not-found response on 404, and a failure response on network or HTTP errors. Registered in `system_administrator` and `full` tool packages.

## Test Results
- Total tests: 1192
- Coverage: 91%
- All passing: yes

## Security
Pre-existing system-level vulnerabilities in pyjwt, setuptools, urllib3, wheel — none introduced by this change.

## Files Changed
- `src/servicenow_mcp/tools/incident_tools.py` — added `DeleteIncidentParams` model and `delete_incident` function
- `src/servicenow_mcp/tools/__init__.py` — import and `__all__` export
- `src/servicenow_mcp/utils/tool_utils.py` — import, param class, and tool definition
- `config/tool_packages.yaml` — added to `system_administrator` and `full` packages
- `tests/test_delete_incident.py` — 13 new tests (sys_id path, number path, 404, network errors, param validation)

## Next Up
Add `get_request_items` tool (sc_req_item table; lists items within an sc_request)
